_meta.rule: CONTENT IS LOCKED. Do not author, edit, reword, or correct any objective, step label, or value. Structural rendering only. Content fixes happen in this file, by Malak.
_meta.disclaimer_note: Study aid only - always follow your school's procedures, your POH, and your instructor.
_meta.structured_questions: One per maneuver, DRAFTED BY CLAUDE from the locked step data. Malak must review each: fix wording/values, then set reviewed: true. Codex must not author, edit, or add questions.

maneuvers.name: Taxiing
maneuvers.objective: To safely and accurately control the airplane during all ground operations.

steps.label: Stop
steps.value: Power to Idle, Equal Pressure on Brakes

structured.prompt: One step is missing from this flow. Which one?

maneuvers.name: Straight and Level
maneuvers.objective: To maintain a constant altitude, heading, and airspeed during coordinated flight.

steps.label: Attitude
steps.value: Establish and Maintain with Primary Flight Controls

steps.label: Trim
steps.value: At Desired Airspeed, to Relieve Control Pressure

maneuvers.name: Climb
maneuvers.objective: To maintain the flight attitude, airspeed, and coordination for best altitude gain with climb power.

steps.label: Attitude
steps.value: Establish and Maintain at Vx, Vy, or Cruise Climb with Primary Flight Controls

steps.label: Trim
steps.value: At Desired Airspeed, to Relieve Control Pressure

steps.label: Recover
steps.value: Level Off at Assigned Altitude with Coordinated Use of Flight Controls

maneuvers.name: Descent
maneuvers.objective: To maintain the flight attitude, airspeed, and coordination for desired descent.

steps.label: Attitude
steps.value: Establish and Maintain for Descent with Primary Flight Controls

steps.label: Trim
steps.value: At Desired Airspeed, to Relieve Control Pressure

steps.label: Recover
steps.value: Level Off at Assigned Altitude with Coordinated Use of Flight Controls

maneuvers.name: Level Off
maneuvers.objective: To return the airplane to straight and level coordinated flight following a climb or descent.

steps.label: Attitude
steps.value: Establish and Maintain Straight and Level Flight with Primary Flight Controls

steps.label: Attitude
steps.value: Establish and Maintain with Primary Flight Controls

structured.prompt: Leveling off from a descent — power?

maneuvers.name: Level Turn
maneuvers.objective: To maintain altitude, coordination, and airspeed while holding a constant bank angle in either direction.

steps.label: Attitude
steps.value: Establish and Maintain with Primary Flight Controls

steps.label: Recover
steps.value: Rollout on Assigned Heading with Coordinated Use of Flight Controls

maneuvers.name: Climbing Turn
maneuvers.objective: To maintain the flight attitude for a coordinated turn in climbing flight.

steps.label: Attitude
steps.value: Establish and Maintain with Primary Flight Controls

steps.label: Trim
steps.value: At Desired Airspeed, to Relieve Control Pressure

steps.label: Recover
steps.value: On Assigned Heading and Altitude with Coordinated Use of Flight Controls

maneuvers.name: Descending Turn
maneuvers.objective: To maintain the flight attitude for a coordinated turn in descending flight.

steps.label: Attitude
steps.value: Establish and Maintain with Primary Flight Controls

steps.label: Trim
steps.value: At Desired Airspeed, to Relieve Control Pressure

steps.label: Recover
steps.value: On Assigned Heading and Altitude with Coordinated Use of Flight Controls

maneuvers.name: Slow Flight (Clean)
maneuvers.objective: To establish, maintain, and maneuver the airplane while coordinated at airspeeds and configurations required for takeoffs, landings, and go-arounds.

steps.label: Power
steps.value: Mixture Rich; Throttle Full; Carburetor Heat Off

structured.prompt: Slow flight (clean) — pitch to maintain altitude until what speed?

maneuvers.name: Slow Flight (Dirty)
maneuvers.objective: To establish, maintain, and maneuver the airplane while coordinated at airspeeds and configurations required for takeoffs, landings, and go-arounds.

steps.label: Configuration
steps.value: Verify Below Vfe; Add Full Flaps in 10° Increments

steps.label: Power
steps.value: Mixture Rich; Throttle Full; Carburetor Heat Off

steps.label: Configuration
steps.value: Flaps 20° Immediately; At 55 Knots Flaps 10°; At 67 Knots Flaps 0°

structured.prompt: Flap retraction schedule on slow flight (dirty) recovery?
options: Flaps 20° Immediately; At 55 Knots Flaps 10°; At 67 Knots Flaps 0°
Hold Full Flaps Until 67 Knots, Then Flaps 0°

maneuvers.name: Power-Off Stall
maneuvers.objective: To familiarize the pilot with the conditions that produce stalls during landing, to assist in recognizing an approaching stall, and to develop the habit of taking prompt and corrective action for recovery.

steps.label: Configuration
steps.value: Verify Below Vfe; Add Full Flaps in 10° Increments

steps.label: Power
steps.value: Mixture Rich; Throttle Full; Carburetor Heat Off

steps.label: Configuration
steps.value: Flaps 20° Immediately; At 55 Knots and Positive Rate Flaps 10°; At 67 Knots and Positive Rate Flaps 0°

maneuvers.name: Power-On Stall
maneuvers.objective: To familiarize the pilot with the conditions that produce stalls during takeoff, to assist in recognizing an approaching stall, and to develop the habit of taking prompt and corrective action for recovery.

steps.label: Pitch
steps.value: Slight Nose High Attitude and Maintain Until Stall Occurs

steps.label: Power
steps.value: Mixture Rich; Throttle Full; Carburetor Heat Off

steps.label: Configuration
steps.value: Verify Positive Rate; 67 Knots Verify Flaps 0°

structured.prompt: Power-on stall entry — power at 60 knots?

maneuvers.name: Steep Turns
maneuvers.objective: To complete two 360° turns with proper control technique using 45° of bank while maintaining altitude, coordination, orientation, and division of attention.

steps.label: Attitude
steps.value: Increase Back Pressure to Maintain Altitude

steps.label: Attitude
steps.value: Reduce Bank 20° Before Target Heading; Reduce Back Pressure Through 30° Bank

structured.prompt: Steep turns — when do you begin the rollout?

maneuvers.name: Rectangular Course
maneuvers.objective: To maintain a rectangular course while correcting for wind and maintaining altitude. Attention should be divided between airplane control, ground track, and maintaining coordinated flight.

steps.label: Field
steps.value: 1 Mile x 1/2 Mile; Oriented With the Wind; Safe for Landing

steps.label: Entry
steps.value: Midfield 45° to Downwind; Establish Downwind 1/4 Mile Away

steps.label: Rollout
steps.value: Abeam Field Boundary, 1/4 Mile Away; With Wind Correction as Needed

structured.prompt: Rectangular course — how do you enter?
options: On a Base-Equivalent Leg, 1/2 Mile Away
Directly Over the Field, Then Circle
Midfield 45° to Downwind; Establish Downwind 1/4 Mile Away

maneuvers.name: Turns Around a Point
maneuvers.objective: To maintain a circular ground track with a uniform radius from a point while correcting for wind drift and maintaining altitude. Attention should be divided between airplane control, ground track, and maintaining coordinated flight.

steps.label: Entry
steps.value: Downwind 1/4 Mile to Right of Point

steps.label: Bank
steps.value: Adjust to Maintain Constant Radius; 45° Maximum Bank

structured.prompt: Turns around a point — how do you enter?
options: Downwind 1/4 Mile to Right of Point
Upwind 1/4 Mile to Left of Point
Downwind 1 Mile to Right of Point

maneuvers.name: S-Turns Across a Road
maneuvers.objective: To maintain a ground track with ground references while correcting for wind drift and maintaining altitude. Attention should be divided between airplane control, ground track, and maintaining coordinated flight.

steps.label: Road
steps.value: At Least 2 Miles, Perpendicular to Wind

steps.label: Entry
steps.value: Downwind, First Turn is to Left

steps.label: Bank
steps.value: Adjust to Maintain 1/4 Mile Semicircle; 45° Maximum Bank

structured.prompt: S-turns — what road do you pick?
options: At Least 2 Miles, Perpendicular to Wind
At Least 2 Miles, Parallel to Wind

maneuvers.name: Normal Takeoff
maneuvers.objective: To make a smooth and coordinated transition from the runway to climbing flight.

maneuvers.name: Crosswind Takeoff
maneuvers.objective: To maintain directional control during ground roll and lift off, then maintain a pitch attitude and airspeed which will give the best altitude gain while tracking out on the runway centerline.

steps.label: Wind Correction Angle
steps.value: Into the Wind to Maintain Centerline

maneuvers.name: Soft-Field Takeoff
maneuvers.objective: To set and maintain an attitude where the airplane will lift-off at the slowest possible speed then transition into ground effect before climbing.

steps.label: Attitude
steps.value: Set, Then Maintain by Reducing Elevator Pressure During Acceleration

structured.prompt: One step is missing from this flow. Which one?

maneuvers.name: Short-Field Takeoff
maneuvers.objective: To get the airplane airborne in the shortest distance possible and climb to clear obstacles where takeoff area is limited.

steps.label: Attitude
steps.value: Vx Until 50-ft Obstacle Cleared, then Vy

options: Vy Until 50 ft. Obstacle Cleared, then Vx
Vx Until 50 ft. Obstacle Cleared, then Vy
Vx All the Way to Pattern Altitude

maneuvers.name: Traffic Pattern
maneuvers.objective: To safely and accurately maneuver the airplane on each leg of the traffic pattern in preparation for landing.

steps.label: Enter
steps.value: Midfield 45° to Downwind Leg at Traffic Pattern Altitude

steps.label: Downwind Leg
steps.value: Maintain 80-85 Knots; Track Parallel to Runway While Approximately 1/4 - 1/2 Mile From Runway

steps.label: Abeam Landing Point or Traffic to Follow
steps.value: Carburetor Heat Verify On; 1500 RPM; Verify Below Vfe, Flaps 10°; Airspeed 75 Knots - TRIM

steps.label: Base Leg
steps.value: Turn at 45° From Landing Point; Wind Correction as Required; 20° Flaps; Airspeed 70 Knots - TRIM

steps.label: Final
steps.value: Turn to Align with Runway Centerline; Wind Correction as Required; 30° Flaps; Airspeed 65 Knots - TRIM

maneuvers.name: Normal Landing
maneuvers.objective: To safely and accurately transition the airplane from descending flight to touchdown on the main wheels and over the runway centerline.

steps.label: Airspeed
steps.value: Maintain 65 Knots with Simultaneous Pitch and Power

steps.label: Glidepath
steps.value: Maintain with Simultaneous Pitch and Power

steps.label: Level Off
steps.value: With Proper Visual Cue Over Runway Centerline

steps.label: Roundout
steps.value: With Proper Visual Cue Over Runway Centerline

steps.label: Touchdown
steps.value: On Main Wheels and Over Runway Centerline

steps.label: Exit Runway
steps.value: At Earliest Location and Safe Speed

maneuvers.name: Crosswind Landing
maneuvers.objective: To safely and accurately transition the airplane from descending flight to touchdown on the main wheels and over the runway centerline during various crosswind conditions.

steps.label: Final Approach
steps.value: Upwind Wing Low, Downwind Rudder To Maintain Runway Centerline

steps.label: Airspeed
steps.value: Maintain 65 Knots with Simultaneous Pitch and Power

steps.label: Glidepath
steps.value: Maintain with Simultaneous Pitch and Power

steps.label: Level Off and Roundout
steps.value: Same Visual Cues as Normal Landing

steps.label: Touchdown
steps.value: Upwind Wheel First and Over Runway Centerline

steps.label: Rollout
steps.value: Smoothly Apply Wind Correction and Decelerate on Runway Centerline

steps.label: Exit Runway
steps.value: At Earliest Location and Safe Speed

structured.prompt: Crosswind landing — control technique on final?
options: Crab With No Correction Through Touchdown

maneuvers.name: Soft-Field Landing
maneuvers.objective: To safely and accurately transition the airplane from descending flight to touchdown on the main wheels on soft or rough fields.

steps.label: Airspeed
steps.value: Maintain 60-65 Knots with Simultaneous Pitch and Power

steps.label: Glidepath
steps.value: Maintain with Simultaneous Pitch and Power

steps.label: Level Off
steps.value: With Proper Visual Cue Over Runway Centerline

steps.label: Roundout
steps.value: With Proper Visual Cue Over Runway Centerline

steps.label: Touchdown
steps.value: As Softly as Possible On Main Wheels and Over Runway Centerline

steps.label: Rollout
steps.value: Gradually Increase Elevator Pressure while Decelerating On Runway Centerline

steps.label: Exit Runway
steps.value: At Earliest Location and Safe Speed

maneuvers.name: Short-Field Landing
maneuvers.objective: To safely and accurately transition the airplane from descending flight to touchdown on the main wheels over a 50 foot obstacle and stop in the shortest distance possible.

steps.label: Airspeed
steps.value: Maintain 60-63 Knots with Simultaneous Pitch and Power

steps.label: Glidepath
steps.value: Maintain with Simultaneous Pitch and Power

steps.label: Level Off and Roundout
steps.value: With Minimal Float at Proper Visual Cue Over Runway Centerline

steps.label: Touchdown
steps.value: On Main Wheels and Over Runway Centerline

steps.label: Rollout
steps.value: Promptly Retract Flaps, Gradually Increase Elevator Pressure and Use Maximum Effective Braking while Decelerating On Runway Centerline

steps.label: Exit Runway
steps.value: At Earliest Location and Safe Speed

maneuvers.name: Go-Around
maneuvers.objective: To transition the airplane from the approach descent to climb attitude in preparation for another landing.

steps.label: Power
steps.value: Mixture Rich; Throttle Full; Carburetor Heat Off

steps.label: Attitude
steps.value: Smoothly Establish and Maintain at Vy

steps.label: Configuration
steps.value: Flaps 20° Immediately; At 55 Knots and Positive Rate Flaps 10°; At 67 Knots and Positive Rate Flaps 0°

steps.label: Climb
steps.value: Continue in Traffic Pattern for Landing

maneuvers.name: Forward Slip
maneuvers.objective: To steepen the approach angle using opposite control forces.

steps.label: Recover
steps.value: Return to Normal Descent when on Glidepath

maneuvers.name: Emergency Approach and Landing (Simulated)
maneuvers.objective: To recognize and react to an emergency situation in such a manner as to safeguard life and minimize damage to the airplane. Attention should be focused on airplane control, following recommended procedures, and selecting a suitable landing site.

steps.label: Airspeed
steps.value: 65 Knots Flaps Up; 60 Knots Flaps Down

steps.label: Best Field
steps.value: Into the Wind, Free from Obstructions; Minimal Geographic Features

maneuvers.name: Emergency Descent
maneuvers.objective: To descend as smoothly and rapidly as possible to a lower altitude or to the ground for an emergency landing, while not exceeding the structural limits of the airplane.

steps.label: Power
steps.value: Mixture Rich; Carb Heat On; Throttle Idle

steps.label: Attitude
steps.value: Simultaneously pitch down to maintain Vfe -5 kts

steps.label: Maneuver
steps.value: Roll wings level ~150 feet above recovery altitude

steps.label: Attitude
steps.value: Smoothly pitch level for target altitude

steps.label: Power
steps.value: Mixture Rich; Throttle Full; Carb Heat Off

steps.label: Configuration
steps.value: Flaps 20° immediately; Positive climb, Flaps 10 at Vx; Positive rate, Flaps 0 at Vy

structured.prompt: Emergency descent — the maneuver is flown with what bank?

maneuvers.name: Diversion
maneuvers.objective: To compute course, distance, time, and fuel from last known position to an alternate airport in preparation for an unplanned landing.

steps.label: Line
steps.value: Draw From Current Location to Destination

steps.label: Course
steps.value: Position Straight Edge Parallel to Course Line; Reposition Over Compass Rose; Determine Course TO Airport

steps.label: Distance
steps.value: Compute Using Rule of Thumb Calculations

steps.label: Time
steps.value: Compute Using Rule of Thumb Calculations

steps.label: Fuel
steps.value: Compute Using Rule of Thumb Calculations

maneuvers.name: Lost Procedures
maneuvers.objective: To determine geographic position in flight after becoming disoriented.

steps.label: Call for Help
steps.value: Last ATC Frequency; Known ATC Frequency; FSS Frequency; Flightwatch 122.00; Emergency Frequency 121.50
